diagrams.title: Peterson's Algorithm Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how two processes P0 and P1 coordinate using turn and flag variables to achieve mutual exclusion
elements: Process P0 entry: Set flag[0]=TRUE, turn=1, check while(flag[1] && turn==1)
Process P1 entry: Set flag[1]=TRUE, turn=0, check while(flag[0] && turn==0)
diagrams.purpose: Understand software-based mutual exclusion for two processes

diagrams.title: Semaphore Operations
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how wait() and signal() modify semaphore value and manage waiting queue
elements: Semaphore value starts at N (for counting)
wait() decrements: if value < 0, process added to waiting queue and blocked
signal() increments: if value <= 0, wake one process from waiting queue
Negative semaphore value = number of waiting processes
diagrams.purpose: Visualize semaphore behavior and blocking/waking mechanism

diagrams.title: Circular Buffer for Producer-Consumer
diagrams.description: Shows a circular buffer of size N with 'in' and 'out' pointers moving cyclically
elements: in pointer: where producer adds next item
out pointer: where consumer removes next item
diagrams.purpose: Understand circular buffer management and overflow/underflow conditions

diagrams.title: Dining Philosophers States
diagrams.description: Five philosophers at round table with fork between each pair, showing three states: thinking, hungry, eating
elements: Five lines for forks between them
State transitions: THINKING -> HUNGRY -> EATING -> THINKING
Philosopher i can use forks i and (i+1)%5
Deadlock when all pick up left fork simultaneously
diagrams.purpose: Visualize resource allocation and deadlock scenarios
